persona.name: Consistent Builder
persona.description: Someone who works on their side project before the day takes over, ships something small every week, and treats the work as a standing appointment — not a someday.

milestones.title: Ship the first version
milestones._comment: 20 of 21 — checking this off during the take completes it on camera

action.title: Work on the project for 30 minutes
action.anchorLink: After I pour my morning coffee
action.kickstartVersion: Open the project and write one line of code

action.title: Post one update about what I built
action.anchorLink: After I close my laptop for the day
action.kickstartVersion: Post a single screenshot, no caption needed

action.title: Send one message to a potential user
action.kickstartVersion: Draft the message — you don't have to send it yet

_comment_history: missDaysAgo = every action missed that day (red on the calendar — proves 'a missed day never erases you'). partialDaysAgo = only the first scheduled action done (amber). Today is deliberately left unlogged so the demo can complete it on camera.

notes.3: Shipped the settings screen — felt easy today
notes.6: Only had 20 minutes but it counted

reflections.userInput: I'm ready for my weekly review.
reflections.aiFeedback: You showed up on four of five mornings and shipped the settings screen — that's the Consistent Builder doing exactly what they do. The 30-minute block before the day starts is clearly working. Let's protect it.

reflections.userInput: I'm ready for my weekly review.
reflections.aiFeedback: A lighter week, and that's information, not failure. The mornings you did start, you kept going — so the friction is starting, not continuing. Next week, try the two-minute version on the hard days.

onboardingAnswers: I want to finally launch the side project I've been putting off — an app I've been sketching for over a year.
Mornings before work are realistic. I'd rather do 20 focused minutes than pretend I'll do two hours.
